ticket.title: Move SQLite path out of hard-coded `~/.taskflow/db.sqlite`
ticket.description:
Operators want to run multiple TaskFlow instances on one host and need to point each at a separate SQLite file. The path is currently hard-coded.

A prior specifier draft exists on the change branch (see prior_draft below). The operator left a comment asking us to also cover the WebDAV sync path which references the same hard-coded location.

priorDraft.content:
# Configurable SQLite path (DRAFT 1)

Add TASKFLOW_DB env var. Done.

operatorComments: Don't forget the sync module also references ~/.taskflow/db.sqlite directly — see packages/server/src/sync.ts.
recordedFinalText:
{"files":[{"path":"proposal.md","content":"# Configurable SQLite path (DRAFT 2)

## Problem

The SQLite path is hard-coded in two places:

1. `packages/server/src/db.ts` — primary DB connection.
2. `packages/server/src/sync.ts` — WebDAV sync target.

This blocks running multiple TaskFlow instances on one host.

## Approach

Introduce a single config field `database.path` (default `~/.taskflow/db.sqlite`), readable from `~/.taskflow/config.toml` and overridable via `TASKFLOW_DB`. Both db.ts and sync.ts consume this same resolved path.

## Migration

None required: existing operators see the same default. Operators with custom paths run `tf migrate` once after upgrade."},{"path":"tasks.md","content":"- [ ] Add `database.path` to config schema (packages/shared/config.ts)
- [ ] Resolve config at startup; export resolvedDbPath
- [ ] db.ts uses resolvedDbPath
- [ ] sync.ts uses resolvedDbPath
- [ ] `tf migrate` accepts an explicit --db override
- [ ] Test: two instances with different TASKFLOW_DB do not share state"}],"openQuestions":[],"assumptions":["Tilde expansion happens at config-resolution time, not at use sites"],"risks":["Sync code path is less tested; misconfiguration could silently sync to the default path instead of the configured one — add an integration test that asserts the sync writer points at the resolved path"]}
